steps.title: Spot the tension between appearance and reality
steps.content: The first sentence says the volcanoes “may look similar from afar.” The second sentence reveals that volcanologists “can tell” they are different types based on formation. The key signal is this contrast: what appears similar on the surface is actually different underneath.

steps.title: Choose the word expressing despite
steps.content:
”Though” means “even though” or “despite”—it acknowledges the surface similarity while introducing the deeper truth.

When placed as a parenthetical between subject and verb, it reads: Although the volcanoes may look similar, volcanologists can tell them apart.

The other options fail to acknowledge this contrast: “in addition” adds facts, “therefore” implies causation, and “for example” introduces an illustration.

This matches choice D — D is correct.
